agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement a pure TypeScript function `recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number` that applies bulk discount logic and enforces a $0.01 price floor. This function will be called inline from a high-traffic pricing endpoint (~500 req/sec peak). No external dependencies, no I/O, no state — just a deterministic calculation with a unit test suite.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Final price = base price minus applicable discounts, floored at $0.01. Price can never be zero or negative.","testable_assertion":"For any inputs, recalculatePrice(...) >= 0.01"},{"rule":"Bulk discount of 10% applies when quantity >= 10.","testable_assertion":"recalculatePrice(10, X) === X * 0.9 (when result >= 0.01); recalculatePrice(9, X) === X (when no other discounts apply)"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Pure TypeScript function: recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number","implementation_note":"Stateless, deterministic, no external dependencies. Suitable for inline execution in a hot path at 500 req/sec."},{"decision":"$0.01 floor enforcement","implementation_note":"Apply Math.max(0.01, computedPrice) as the final step after all discount logic. Add a JSDoc comment on the function documenting this floor behavior for on-call engineers."},{"decision":"Unit tests only","implementation_note":"No integration tests needed for a pure function. Cover all acceptance criteria plus edge cases (zero base price, very large discounts, boundary at quantity=9 vs 10)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Bulk discount applied at quantity threshold","given":"quantity=10, basePrice=5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 4.50"},{"criterion":"No discount below threshold","given":"quantity=9, basePrice=5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 5.00"},{"criterion":"Price floor enforced","given":"a discount that would reduce price to $0.00 or below","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 0.01"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"bulk discount","technical_term":"10% multiplier (0.9x)","definition":"A 10% reduction applied to basePrice when quantity >= 10"},{"domain_term":"price floor","technical_term":"Math.max(0.01, computedPrice)","definition":"The minimum allowable final price, ensuring the result is never zero or negative"}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["All three agents agree on function signature, purity, and statelessness","Domain rules and acceptance criteria are fully consistent with each other","No contradictions between domain, architecture, and operational perspectives"],"low_confidence_areas":["Rounding behavior not explicitly specified — standard IEEE 754 floating-point applies unless currency-precision (e.g., two decimal places) is required"],"assumptions":["basePrice is provided in dollars as a positive number","No other discount types exist beyond the bulk discount described","Two-decimal-place rounding (standard currency precision) is expected but not explicitly stated — Shaq should round to 2 decimal places"]}}
